workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just got this from Kevin Lee at Fabrikam Residences -- I need help figuring out security posture. Not urgent but would be good to have by EOD.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: security posture Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-03-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), David (Fabrikam Residences)

[00:02:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:12:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:16:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:19:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:28:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:31:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:33:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:35:00] David (Fabrikam Residences): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-F trap (Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-F failure mode (Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-F -- Skips manager-decision items when consensus was reached
